item.title: I put 100's of Hours Into Making this Tiny Tina Cosplay
item.description:
I put 100's of hours into making this Tiny Tina cosplay from the new game Tiny Tina's Wonderlands! I started it months ago and have been working on every tiny detail on the costume. Recently I got to wear it all and do a photoshoot of it. Wig and makeup alone takes over an hour to put on since its so makeup heavy with all the lines and all the pieces on the costume itself.
Some photos from the photoshoot. It was freezing when we did this photoshoot - but in the end I think it was worth it.
I even made an axe.
Photo of the skirt I sewed together, destroyed then painted! My favorite part was painting the unicorn piece. I used super tiny paint pens to get all the fine details.
A few weeks ago I shared with you all my pink Borderlands styled shoe. Well I did recently finish the other shoe (Tina is a lunatic so she wears two completely different shoes). Here is what the other shoe looks like!
And a video of the two shoes on. Can you believe that these shoes were the same style at one point? I had to COMPLETELY take them apart and add a whole bunch of pieces to them to make them look the way they do in the game. I even made custom soles.
Timelapse of painting parts on the shoe. This is 2 hours of painting. I mostly use acrylic paint/paint pens.
A few build highlights of my cosplay.
Another 2 hours of painting for the shoe parts. I put so much into this Tiny Tina cosplay - honestly even being at this stage of the build - I want to make more for it. The build challenged me in ways that no other cosplay had and I thoroughly enjoyed just the process of creating. Tiny Tina's Wonderlands comes out later this month and I know I will have more costume pieces and props I want to make from it. If you are curious... my full build log can be found here: https://twitter.com/MangolooCosplay/status/1432778173939339266 And more of what I am working on at https://www.instagram.com/mangoloo.cos/
item.dc:creator: Mangoloo

item.title: Dump of street musicians
item.description:
#1 Allie Sherlock ~ Stephen's Green Dublin
#2 Jubilee Stomp ~ Royal Street, New Orleans
#3 Spanish street musician Borja Catanesi and the 68 year old dancer from The Hague Mr Roland Parijs ~ The Hague, Netherlands
#4 Amsterdam ~ Jack Broadbent
#5 Jamming with street players ~ Florence
#6 Santiago Moreno ~ Buenos Aires, Argentina
#7 A castle’s melody – Moses Concas
#8 Light in Babylon / Hinech Yava ~ Istanbul
#9 A Roman teen playing covers of Ennio Morricone's music from his terrace above a deserted Piazza Navona Roma during the coronavirus lockdown
#10 Jackson Square, New Orleans
item.dc:creator: vidadobl6

item.title: My first post. Putin, you total piece of warmongering shit. My heart breaks for Ukraine and the civilian casualties of a war YOU are singularly responsible for.
item.description:
Maria, Fedora and Baby Kirill rush to hospital.
They grieve their 18 month old baby.
They grieve not being able to save Kirill.

Me, when she tells me no one is home. Welcome to the dump everyone, hope your weekend is shaping up well. Check your wifi and I you enjoyed this very assorted dump of videos.

item.dc:creator: hoopsnek

item.title: Squidward trumps.
item.description:
FP Edit - THANK YOU EVERYONE FOR THE FEEDBACK, AND THE KIND WORDS. I began painting to help with my anxiety, and my depression. It has definitely helped. I'm having fun, I'm being productive - it's been a saving grace. I can't wait to produce more! Thanks again everyone! ------------------------------ I began trying to paint in December 2021... the fruition of Handsome Squidward. I'd like to share some of my other pieces with Imgur.. the first 6 are acrylic, the last 2 are oils. Please be nice - but all feedback and opinions are welcome, and encouraged.
#2
#3
#4
#5
#6
#7
#8
#9
#10
item.dc:creator: oldfishnewfish

item.dc:creator: DaniusJones123

item.title: [oc] my freshly painted rainbow shelf inspired my my 90’s Polly Pockets!
item.description:
Before - ugly dark teal made the whole room feel dark and heavy
Starting the first coat of primer
This was the first coat of primer - it took two coats to make it light enough to paint over with the pastel paint.
Some of my Polly Pocket compacts that I color matched at the store.
I felt like the Polly compacts I chose were a bit too dark for my liking, so i created a pastel palette based on other Polly compacts.
The lighting is off on this but these matched up pretty well.
I was going to have 3 sets of 6 alternating rainbow colors, but I felt like the bands were too narrow at roughly 4.5” tall stripes. If I did 2 sets of 6 alternating colors, it would wind up being 6.5” tall stripes. That felt too wide. I compromised and did 5.5” tall stripes, which was absolutely the best choice.
Taping took soooo loooong
I did a “crumb coat” of primer over the painters tape to create a leak barrier and ensure a crisp line.
I started with the cotton candy pink color first
Then the buttercup yellow
Finally the aqua
Kinda almost wanted to stop here because I loved how it looked but I bought all 6 paints so I had to keep going!
Taping the stripes for the secondary colors proved to be sooo much easier!
Crumb coat for these as well. This was actually the most important part.
Filling in with peach
Now mint goes on
Finally the lavender
Seriously impressed.
I love how crisp the lines were when I peeled the tape off!
The gorgeous pastel rainbow finished product!
item.dc:creator: CatsRuleEverythingAroundMeeee

item.title: Teachers are more than just information dumpers
item.description: Where I'm from, teachers are under being demonized by the government as ideology pushers. It's not right. Teachers do so much, they care so much, and we need to stand up for them.
